description_long: This object is an electric guitar with a classic solid body design, featuring a dark blue or black finish on the body and a white pickguard. The neck is made of a lighter wood, likely maple, and extends from the body with a visible fretboard and metallic frets. The headstock is equipped with six tuning pegs arranged in a single row, typical of many Fender-style guitars. The body includes two visible pickups, a control plate with knobs, and a bridge, all in metallic finishes. The overall shape is reminiscent of the iconic Telecaster, with a single cutaway for easier access to higher frets. The guitar's surface appears smooth and glossy, reflecting light subtly.
description: A solid-body electric guitar with a dark blue body, white pickguard, and light wood neck, featuring metallic hardware and a single-cutaway design.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full length of the guitar, highlighting the body shape, pickguard, and neck.
description_view_1: View from the headstock end, emphasizing the tuning pegs and the narrow profile of the neck.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the thickness of the body and the alignment of the neck with the body.
description_view_3: View from the body end, showing the pickguard, control plate, and bridge area in detail.
attribute_headstock_design: Flat headstock with six inline tuning pegs, no visible logo, classic Fender-style contour
